# Support

## Where to go for what

| You want to... | Go here |
|---|---|
| Report a bug | Open an issue using `ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml` |
| Request a feature | Open an issue using `ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.yml` |
| Ask a question, discuss a design idea, or float something not ready to be a concrete change | **GitHub Discussions** — see `DISCUSSION_TEMPLATE/README.md` for categories |
| Report a Code of Conduct concern | See `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`'s enforcement section — do not use a public issue |
| Report a security vulnerability | Contact maintainers privately — do not open a public issue (see `security.yml` for the automated scanning that supplements this) |

## Why issues and discussions are split

Issues are for concrete, actionable items with a clear "done" state. Discussions are for open-ended questions and ideas that aren't ready to be tracked as work yet. Filing a question as an issue tends to leave it open indefinitely with no clear resolution — Discussions keeps the issue tracker meaningful as an actual to-do list.

## Response expectations

Mendel is run under a BDFL governance model (see `GOVERNANCE.md`) by maintainer/volunteer time, not a supported commercial product — there's no guaranteed response time. Issues that follow the templates and discussions that are clearly scoped get triaged fastest.

## Before you ask

Many "why does X work this way" questions are already answered in the relevant module's `README.md` or in `docs/` — worth a quick check first.
